description:
Goal
Remove dead production-facing compatibility entrypoints left behind by the compiler-spine refactor so the main build stays warning-free while preserving the test harnesses that still exercise those seams.

Signal
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` passes after the compiler-spine branch is pushed.
- Test-only compatibility helpers in `src/compiler.rs` and `src/ir_codegen` are gated to tests instead of remaining dead in production.
- No production caller regresses to the old codegen-owned driver path.

Strategy
1. Confirm which helpers are now test-only after the refactor.
2. Gate those helpers with `#[cfg(test)]` rather than carrying dead production code.
3. Re-run fmt, clippy, and targeted tests, then push the fix and re-check PR CI.

References
- `src/compiler.rs`
- `src/ir_codegen/mod.rs`
- `src/ir_codegen/driver.rs`
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
title: Compiler spine: prune test-only codegen compatibility shims
